title: Concerning Shinji and The Dead Sea Scrolls
display_title: Concerning Shinji and The Dead Sea Scrolls
wikitext:
[]: Concerning Shinji and The Dead Sea Scrolls             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon May 12, 2014 7:47 pm I was writing this fanfic and a thought occurred to me, how would Shinji have reacted to reading them? I know this is just another thought experiment but I can't stop pondering this stuff. How would he deal with the fact that the only reason he was conceived was so he could be broken down and manipulated into catering to his parents wants and desires? After reading them would he still believe he had free will? How about Love or any emotion at all? All those loved ones that he ended up killing, getting killed, or having them sacrifice themselves for him were just pawns, they had no other purpose than to help break him. How would he feel about the fact that his parents knew everything? Fuck man, I know you guys have probably thought about all this before but holy shit

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon May 12, 2014 7:57 pm You're already writing in many expectations into DSS that aren't justified on Eva alone. DSS predictions weren't infallible for one and they have no relevance on the question of free will. Secondly, they clearly weren't very precise, definetly not to a degree Gendo and Yui could've "planned everything" ahead. Rest is even crazier. Like, what the fuck. There's zero reason to think (at least in original) that Gendo and Yui planned Shinji's life ahead based on some mystical mumbo etc. Everything that you write reeks of crazy fanfic so there's no real to consult us or original series on minor detail like this.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon May 12, 2014 7:57 pm Er, he'd probably be like "what am I even reading this is boring and indecipherable as all hell" because they're probably incomprehensible without a lifetime's understanding of Brabantic philology and Kabbalah and metaphysical biology and such. And what the hell are you going on about, Yui gave birth to Shinji because she wanted to have a baby. She did what she did for him, not the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Mon May 12, 2014 8:25 pm Shinji wasn't born to serve his parents...He was three when Yui and Gendo began manipulating SEELE's scenario. I just noticed, but does the DSS in DSS Choker stand for dead sea scroll?

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon May 12, 2014 8:30 pm Firetrout wrote: Shinji wasn't born to serve his parents...He was three when Yui and Gendo began manipulating SEELE's scenario. I just noticed, but does the DSS in DSS Choker stand for dead sea scroll?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon May 12, 2014 8:31 pm https://wiki.evageeks.org/DSS_Choker

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Mon May 12, 2014 8:34 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Oh snap I was under the impression that they met Seele before Second Impact, if that's the case my worries were for nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon May 12, 2014 8:54 pm @ Xard Good Sir what are you talking about? Crazy Stuff? You mean my theories on the Rebuild stuff? The only other things I can think of is this and the Asuka thread, which I don't view as crazy, it's just a thought experiment on how they would react in situation given what we know about their character, you know chit chat non serious stuff. @ Lavinius Actually I didn't think any of that stuff would be involved with it, all the religious stuff is there just for the rule of cool, the actual discussed stuff would probably have to do with Ancient Astronaut crap but to me it's not important how he read it, it's just him understanding it and the after math that I am pondering. Yui could have wanted a baby for many reasons Good Sir. @ Firetrout I have no idea what the DSS choker stands for Good Sir, maybe Decommissioning Sync Severance/Severing? Deadly Sync Severance/Severing?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon May 12, 2014 10:06 pm I can't remember: do we actually ever see the Dead Sea Scrolls? I mean, are they an actual, readable document, or something more esoteric? Terms were applied to several artifacts and items within Evangelion that did not necessarily match the term. In that case, could Shinji even comprehend what he's looking at? Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon May 12, 2014 10:11 pm ^ I could have sworn we saw a flash back of them, if I recall they looked like a messed up folder made out of stone.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon May 12, 2014 10:16 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: @ Xard Good Sir what are you talking about? Crazy Stuff? You mean my theories on the Rebuild stuff? The only other things I can think of is this and the Asuka thread, which I don't view as crazy, it's just a thought experiment on how they would react in situation given what we know about their character, you know chit chat non serious stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon May 12, 2014 10:39 pm ^ Oh Good Sir I thought the Dead Sea Scrolls were the only source of info in that Universe that spoke of the Evas, the Angels, and the Impacts. If there are more could you please tell me? It would be greatly appreciated. To keep it on topic, I personally feel that he would take it very badly, especially if it occurred after 3I, when he felt he had a firm grasp on his reality.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 13, 2014 3:29 am Wait, are you talking about the NGE or the Rebuild continuity? In NGE Shinji wasn't part at all of Gendo and Yui's plans, he was just called because Rei was severely injured and Gendo didn't had any other choice. And while it's true that in Rebuild Shinji was part of Gendo's plan since the beginning, so far we don't have indications that Yui planed to use Shinji too. Also, the SDSS are more than probably written in the FAR language, so unless he find a translated version instead of the real deal, he won't understand anything of what he has in his hands. But in any case, the SDSS are not a prophecy, they are an instruction manual about how works the FAR technology used in the Black/White Moon and the Seeds of Life : how do they work, what's their goal (terraform the planet to make it suitable for lifeforms based on them), what's the big spear they carry and what it does, that the ADAM-type SoL will create 15-16 Angels (it was never clear how much Kaworu's creation was planed by the SDSS instructions) with the Fruit of Life and the Lilith-type will create a virtually infinite number of Lilin (humans) with the Fruit of Knowledge, and that the two Fruits should never be fused or shit will hit the fan hard. From here SEELE extrapolated and built their scenario, they talk about mankind's destiny and the prophecy of the SDSS because they're a bunch of crazy mystics, nothing more. But Shinji's fate was never in the SDSS. Firetrout wrote: I just noticed, but does the DSS in DSS Choker stand for dead sea scroll?

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Tue May 13, 2014 9:17 am ElMariachi wrote: In NGE Shinji wasn't part at all of Gendo and Yui's plans, he was just called because Rei was severely injured and Gendo didn't had any other choice.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 13, 2014 9:27 am amitakartok wrote: Negative on that. Gendo specifically wanted Shinji to fulfill his plans, maintaining the illusion that Shinji had free will and could've left any time because the best system of control is the one where the subject doesn't even realize he's being controlled - in this case, the controller engineering circumstances so that the subject can't get what he wants without giving the controller what the controller wants.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Tue May 13, 2014 10:04 am ElMariachi wrote: In Rebuild yes, but in NGE Gendo wanted Shinji to have nothing to do with him, because eh was sure that the only thing he could do to his son is harm.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue May 13, 2014 5:58 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: I can't remember: do we actually ever see the Dead Sea Scrolls? I mean, are they an actual, readable document, or something more esoteric?

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Wed May 14, 2014 12:19 am Shinji can't read old Hebrew or whatever other language the DSS are written in.

[]:             Quote Post by tzigi » Wed May 14, 2014 1:51 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Yar. Right here, in the series OP: http://wiki.evageeks.org/FGC:OP_Cut_085

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 14, 2014 11:23 am Gob Hobblin wrote: I can't remember: do we actually ever see the Dead Sea Scrolls? I mean, are they an actual, readable document, or something more esoteric?

How would he deal with the fact that the only reason he was conceived was so he could be broken down and manipulated into catering to hi
